summary: This is a very brief teaser post directing readers to Joel's column in Inc. Magazine about Fog Creek Software's search for office space. The post highlights the frustration of a failed real estate deal with dry humor, noting that a floor they wanted to expand into turned out not to be available. It also promotes the upcoming Business of Software conference with an early registration reminder.
key_points: Fog Creek Software's office expansion plans were delayed when a deal for additional space fell through
Joel was writing a regular column for Inc. Magazine about running a software company
The post serves primarily as a pointer to external content rather than a standalone essay
money_quotes: We lost some time because a deal to expand at our current location fell through — it turned out that the extra floor we wanted wasn't actually, to use the real estate jargon, 'available.'
key_insight: Even well-run software companies face mundane operational setbacks like failed real estate deals that can slow growth plans.
